## Summary

Expands the Methodology page from a compact overview into a fuller explanation of how RevisionGrade evaluates manuscripts.

## Why

The deficiency roadmap calls out Methodology as still too thin. It needs to explain the 13 criteria, evaluation modes, evidence-backed diagnosis, long-form continuity, and the Evaluate → Revise bridge.

## Changes

- Reframes the page around stable criteria, traceable evidence, and author-controlled revision.
- Expands the four reading principles:
  - Criteria-led reading
  - Evidence before verdict
  - Long-form continuity
  - Revision restraint
- Expands evaluation modes:
  - Short-Form Evaluation: under 25,000 words, 13 criteria only
  - Long-Form Evaluation: 25,000+ words, manuscript-scale readiness
  - Long-Form Multi-Layer Evaluation: deep architecture review for complex manuscripts
- Adds one-line definitions for all 13 story criteria.
- Adds an evidence model:
  - Observation
  - Evidence anchor
  - Reader effect
  - Revision implication
- Adds examples contrasting generic feedback with evidence-backed diagnosis.
- Adds an Evaluate → Revise workflow bridge.
- Adds method boundaries so the page does not overpromise Golden Spine/WAVE analysis for short-form work.

## Scope

Public copy/page structure only.

No auth, database, evaluation, scoring, report, Revise runtime, TrustedPath runtime, Storygate runtime, Agent Readiness runtime, or pipeline behavior changed.
